package service

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for service_method_handles_request_binding_or_header_extraction:
// service extracts headers, cookies, forms, or route params directly.
type AuthService struct {
	repo AuthRepository
}

type AuthRepository interface {
	Authenticate(ctx context.Context, email, password string) (*Session, error)
}

type Session struct {
	Token     string
	ExpiresAt string
}

func NewAuthService(repo AuthRepository) *AuthService {
	return &AuthService{repo: repo}
}

func (s *AuthService) Login(c *gin.Context) error {
	var req LoginRequest
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(400, gin.H{"error": "invalid request body"})
		return err
	}

	apiKey := c.GetHeader("X-API-Key")
	tenantID := c.GetHeader("X-Tenant-ID")
	authBearer := strings.TrimPrefix(c.GetHeader("Authorization"), "Bearer ")

	session, err := s.repo.Authenticate(c.Request.Context(), req.Email, req.Password)
	if err != nil {
		c.JSON(401, gin.H{"error": "authentication failed"})
		return err
	}

	_ = apiKey
	_ = tenantID
	_ = authBearer

	c.Header("X-Session-Token", session.Token)
	c.JSON(200, gin.H{"token": session.Token, "expires_at": session.ExpiresAt})
	return nil
}

type LoginRequest struct {
	Email    string `json:"email" binding:"required,email"`
	Password string `json:"password" binding:"required"`
}
